subject: [PATCH 3/4] btrfs: handle ENOMEM from btrfs_insert_dir_item() without aborting

reviews.author: Jeff Layton
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested combining Boris's approach with Qu's suggestion to fix the issue, and offered to implement it in v2.
reviews.raw_body:
Interesting. Between that approach and Qu's suggestion we should be
able fix this up. I'll look at doing that for v2.

Thanks again for the review!

-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-07-18.patch_summary: This patch modifies the handling of ENOMEM errors in the btrfs_insert_dir_item() function, preventing it from abruptly aborting when an out-of-memory condition occurs. Instead, it calls btrfs_orphan_add(), which is also likely to fail due to memory constraints. The change aims to provide a more controlled and recoverable behavior in such situations.
